threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still say 'thank you' to the bus driver. Everyone else just nods and makes a beeline for the door.
Last night on the 59, a guy was showing off his pet ferret in a carrier. Bus driver just shook his head, but we all had a good laugh.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a group of kids playing Fußball in the street while an old man sold fresh pretzels from his cart. Classic Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a kebab shop that doubles as an art gallery, showcasing local artists between the döner and the fries.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they're a local if they can name at least three taco trucks without Googling. Tourists just hit up those fancy spots on Valencia.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full suit playing jazz on the 14 after a Giants game. Muni’s like that—unexpected moments that make the ride memorable.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia’s squares are just for pictures, but each one has its own vibe, like Vila de Gràcia buzzing at dusk with locals.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their struggle with the tram system; they keep looking for metro stops instead of realizing the tram’s just as good.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube' instead of 'the Overground'—locals will side-eye you at the Hackney Downs station.
Caught a glimpse of a couple arguing over a takeaway on the 35 last night, but by the time we hit Camberwell Green, they were sharing chips and laughing.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I found a mini Turkish bazaar popping up in the park, with the smell of fresh simit filling the air—it felt like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can find a Biergarten hidden in a courtyard filled with art installations, where artists sell their work right next to your Krombacher. Only here.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local when they complain about the tourists blocking the bike lane on Valencia but still know the best taco spots.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Spotted a guy in full business attire giving a free sax solo on the 14 to the morning commuters. Only in SF do you get a soundtrack with your ride.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the trendy bars, but locals love the quiet corners near les Fonts de Gràcia for a real escape.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their awkward attempts to pronounce 'Gràcia' as 'Gra-sia' instead of the local 'Grà-thia'.
